It's understandable to feel overwhelmed by the idea of change when it seems like such a big mountain to climb. I can hear how much you want to believe in your ability to interact differently with Jamie, but that history makes it hard to envision a new path. When you think about those past experiences that contribute to your doubts, what stands out to you the most?
It sounds like that pattern of feeling let down by yourself really weighs heavily on you. It’s understandable to feel daunted by the idea of change when you’ve experienced setbacks in the past. The frustration over missed chances seems to compound your feelings of being stuck. When you reflect on those moments when you tried to reach out or be more open, what do you think held you back the most in those instances?
